title: AppLovin: Back On Track For Growth, But Advise Buying On Dips
text: Despite the recent rally in 2023, investing in AppLovin stock during corrections could be a winning strategy for long-term investors. When considering the recovery of mobile app advertisement market and AppLovin's competitiveness in its field, the company appears to be back on track for growth. However, policy changes regarding personal information and macro headwinds still pose risks for the company.

title: AppLovin: AdTech Solutions Going From Strength To Strength
text: AppLovin's Software Platform is experiencing rapid growth, supporting a return to solid profitability and healthy cash flow generation. APP's upgraded AXON recommendation engine and improved market conditions appear to be driving a growth reacceleration. Much of this is already priced into the stock, but AppLovin should still do well if it can successfully expand into areas like CTV and on-device advertising.

title: SHAREHOLDER ALERT: Purcell & Lefkowitz LLP Is Investigating AppLovin Corporation for Potential Breaches of Fiduciary Duty By Its Board of Directors

text: AppLovin Corp. shares have made it a recent habit of surging after earnings, and Thursday's move was no exception as analysts applauded a successful rollout of the app-monetization company's artificial-intelligence-based ad-targeting engine.

title: AppLovin surpasses expectations with impressive 2Q financial turnaround

title: Compared to Estimates, AppLovin (APP) Q2 Earnings: A Look at Key Metrics

title: AppLovin (APP) Q2 Earnings and Revenues Surpass Estimates
text: AppLovin (APP) came out with quarterly earnings of $0.22 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $0.08 per share. This compares to loss of $0.06 per share a year ago.

title: Applovin stock skyrockets 20% after company posts surprise profit, higher revenue
text: Shares of Applovin Corp. APP, -2.13% jumped more than 20% in the extended session Wednesday after the software company swung to a profit and reported better-than-expected revenue in its second quarter, pinning the outperformance on a “successful” roll-out of its newest advertising product. Applovin earned $80 million, or 22 cents a share, in the quarter, contrasting with a loss $22 million, or 6 cents a share, in the year-ago period.

title: AppLovin AI Advancements to Accelerate Partner Growth
text: PALO ALTO, Calif.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--AppLovin Corporation (NASDAQ: APP) (“AppLovin”), a leading marketing platform, today announced new AI advancements to its mobile user acquisition (UA) platform, AppDiscovery.
